div1.entryFree: Onset, beginning, setting about: if thou wilt leave me, do not leave me last, when other petty griefs have done their spite, but in the o. come, Sonn. 90, 11. I have a sonnet that will serve the turn to give the o. to thy good advice, Gent. III, 2, 94. and for an o., Titus, to advance thy name, Lavinia will I make my empress, Tit. I, 238. == beginning of a combat: swell in their pride, the o. still expecting, Lucr. 432. the o. and retire of both your armies, John II, 326.
